meanings.definition: make a mess of, destroy or ruin
examples: I botched the dinner and we had to eat out
the pianist screwed up the difficult passage in the second movement

meanings.definition: spoil by behaving clumsily or foolishly
showing lack of skill or aptitude
examples: his fumbling attempt to put up a shelf

meanings.definition: lacking physical movement skills, especially with the hands; ; ; ; - Mary H. Vorse
examples: could scarcely empty a scuttle of ashes, so handless was the poor creature
